pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 6 and 28; configuration is correct
pom.xml line 8 shows artifactId as fix-failing-tests; matches git directory name
BubbleSortTest.java line 1 declares package com.sorts; public class BubbleSortTest at line 13

BubbleSortTest.java contains 4 test methods: testEmptyArray (line 17), testSingleElement (line 27), testUnsortedArray (line 36), testAlreadySorted (line 47). Initially 3 have @Test; all 4 should run after fixes.

Static analysis of BubbleSortTest.java: testEmptyArray fails (Bug 1: assertEquals instead of assertArrayEquals), testSingleElement fails (Bug 2: wrong expected value {99} vs actual {42}), testUnsortedArray fails (Bug 3: return value not captured). testAlreadySorted won't run initially (missing @Test annotation)
README.md states 'The BubbleSort.java implementation is correct and must not be modified'. setup_task.sh line 18 stores MD5 checksum for integrity verification.

pi_items.verified_value: BubbleSort algorithm based on TheAlgorithms/Java (MIT License)
pi_items.source: BubbleSort.java line 5 correctly references TheAlgorithms/Java. However, the TheAlgorithms/Java repository (https://github.com/TheAlgorithms/Java) is licensed under MIT License, not Apache 2.0 (verified via GitHub repository footer).

privileged_info_summary: The fix_failing_tests task uses real, non-synthetic data: a Maven project containing a BubbleSort implementation (package com.sorts) and a test suite with exactly 4 test methods in BubbleSortTest. Initially 3 test methods have @Test annotations and all 3 fail: testEmptyArray fails because it uses assertEquals instead of assertArrayEquals for array comparison; testSingleElement fails due to wrong expected value ({99} instead of {42}); testUnsortedArray fails because the return value of sort() is not captured. The fourth test method, testAlreadySorted, lacks the @Test annotation and thus does not execute initially. The BubbleSort implementation references TheAlgorithms/Java code and must not be modified.
